domains.name: Azure AI Foundry and Application Development

questions.stem:
A team is building a chat application in Azure AI Foundry and needs a project-level container for model deployments, connections, and evaluations for `support-chat-prod`.

The team is doing this after a 40% latency increase in production, without changing the approved data boundary, while preserving audit evidence for the release review, after a failed canary exposed missing rollback signals.

What should they create first?

options.text: An Azure AI Search indexer without a search index
A Prompt Shield policy attached to no application
An Azure AI Foundry project in the appropriate hub or resource context
A Document Intelligence custom extractor only

questions.explanation:
**A** is incorrect because it solves a different lifecycle step and does not satisfy the stated runtime requirement. In this scenario, choosing `An` would not meet the required behavior for `support-chat-prod`. See [Azure AI Foundry projects](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-foundry/how-to/create-projects).

**B** is incorrect because it would add operational work while leaving the documented service capability unused. In this scenario, choosing `A` would not meet the required behavior for `support-chat-prod`. See [Azure AI Foundry projects](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-foundry/how-to/create-projects).

**C** is correct because it provides the required managed workflow while preserving the operational and governance requirements. The option aligns with `support-chat-prod` and the constraints stated in the scenario. See [Azure AI Foundry projects](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-foundry/how-to/create-projects).

**D** is incorrect because it targets storage, ingestion, or monitoring rather than the decision point being tested. In this scenario, choosing `A` would not meet the required behavior for `support-chat-prod`. See [Azure AI Foundry projects](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-foundry/how-to/create-projects).
